summary: Cleaned up the remaining clang strict-prototypes warnings caused by C declarations and definitions with empty parameter lists, while also tightening several callback and helper signatures across Dolphin OS/AR/GX, Melee fighter/stage/player/lb headers, and sysdolphin baselib animation code. The PR mostly converted `foo()` declarations to `foo(void)` for no-argument functions, added real parameter types or dummy parameter names where callbacks were intentionally unused, introduced shared baselib callback typing via `HSD_ObjUpdateFunc` and `HSD_ObjData`, and moved `OSException` into a header so exception handlers use one typed signature. The PR body notes that a few TExp warnings remained.
what_changed: Converted no-argument function declarations and definitions from empty parentheses to explicit `void`, including `EXI2_Init(void)`, `AMC_IsStub(void)`, `OSGetConsoleType(void)`, `OSGetStackPointer(void)`, `Fighter_LoadCommonData(void)`, `Player_InitAllPlayers(void)`, and many baselib/list/sislib/lb prototypes.

Added typed parameters to functions that previously had empty or incomplete callback-style signatures, such as stage callbacks in `grbattle` and `grfigure3` using `HSD_GObj*`, `Vec3*`, `int`, and `HSD_JObj*` parameters.
Introduced `src/dolphin/ar/ar.h` with prototypes for `ARStartDMA` and `ARRegisterDMACallback`, and included it from `arq.c` instead of relying on undeclared external use.
Added `__ARQInterruptServiceRoutine(void)` to `arq.h` and added needed OS interrupt include coverage in `arq.c`.
Moved `OSException` into `OSInit.h` and updated exception handler typedefs and implementations from `__OSException` or `s32` to `OSException` in OS alarm, init, interrupt, and context code.
Added non-MWERKS `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stub coverage for `OSGetStackPointer(void)` and corrected several non-MWERKS stub function names in `OSContext.c` to match the functions they stand in for.
Created shared baselib forward declarations and callback typing in `forward.h`: `HSD_Fog`, `HSD_ObjData`, and `HSD_ObjUpdateFunc(any_t obj, enum_t type, HSD_ObjData* fval)`.

Changed baselib animation update APIs from ad hoc `Event`, `void (*)()`, or `FObjData` callback forms to the shared `HSD_ObjUpdateFunc`/`HSD_ObjData` signature across AObj, FObj, Fog, JObj, LObj, MObj, and RObj code.
Renamed/reshaped `FObjData` into forward-declarable `union _HSD_ObjData` in `fobj.h`, with users updated to `HSD_ObjData*`.
Removed an unprototyped declaration from `item2.h` for `func_8026B7F8()` rather than converting it, implying the declaration was unnecessary or not safely typed in this PR.
Added partial TExp declarations in `texp.h`, while the PR body explicitly says a few TExp strict-prototypes warnings still remained.

Adjusted minor expression/type details while touching affected files, including parenthesizing a `GXFrameBuf.c` bitwise expression and changing some integer constants in `jobj.c` to unsigned suffix form.
smart_moves: Used `void` for true no-argument functions instead of leaving C's old-style unspecified parameter list syntax; this directly targets `-Wstrict-prototypes` without changing call behavior.

For callback slots that really receive arguments, the PR did not silence warnings with `void`; it inferred or reused callback parameter types and added named unused parameters like `arg0`, preserving the actual callback ABI.
Centralized baselib animation callback typing with `HSD_ObjUpdateFunc` instead of continuing many incompatible local `void (*)()` or `Event` declarations.
Made `HSD_ObjData` a forward-declarable union and put its typedef in `forward.h`, reducing include-order pressure for shared animation callback signatures.
Promoted `OSException` into `OSInit.h` so OS exception handlers share one typed public declaration rather than repeating a local enum or using an opaque byte typedef.
Preferred including real headers, such as the new `dolphin/ar/ar.h` and `OSInterrupt.h`, over adding loose `extern` declarations in C files.
Preserved a matching-sensitive unused stack allocation in `grfigure3.c` behind `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with a `@todo Unused stack` note instead of removing the artifact silently.
Left evidence of unresolved scope in the PR body by saying a few TExp functions were still excepted, avoiding an overclaim that all warnings were gone.
decomp_lessons: In C, `func()` is not a no-argument prototype; use `func(void)` when the function takes no parameters, especially in headers included by clang warning builds.
Do not fix strict-prototypes warnings mechanically if the function is used as a callback. A stage or baselib callback may need the real callback signature even if the body ignores its parameters.
Shared callback typedefs are preferable when many systems pass the same update function shape through AObj/FObj/JObj/Fog/LObj/MObj/RObj code.
Forward headers are useful for decomp projects when callback typedefs need opaque struct or union names without pulling large dependency chains into every header.
Exception and interrupt handler signatures should use one public enum/typedef across asm and C fallback implementations, otherwise warning cleanup can expose inconsistent prototypes.
For platform-conditional assembly functions, keep the MWERKS asm signature and the non-MWERKS fallback signature aligned; warning cleanup is a good opportunity to catch mismatched fallback names.
When a local `extern foo()` exists only to paper over a missing header, adding or including the real header is a more reusable fix.
If a matching artifact such as unused stack space is required, hide it under the project's matching guard (`MUST_MATCH`) and document it rather than letting warning cleanup perturb matching.
naming_conventions: No-argument functions use explicit `(void)` in declarations and definitions, e.g. `EXI2_Init(void)`, `HSD_ListInitAllocData(void)`, and `Fighter_LoadCommonData(void)`.
Unused newly-added parameters in unnamed decomp functions were given neutral names such as `arg0`, `arg1`, and `arg2`.
Shared baselib callback type names use the `HSD_` prefix and describe their role: `HSD_ObjUpdateFunc` and `HSD_ObjData`.
Forward-declarable baselib struct/union tags follow the existing `_HSD_*` tag style, such as `struct _HSD_Fog` and `union _HSD_ObjData`.
The OS exception enum uses a public `OSException` type name with constants like `OS_EXCEPTION_FLOATING_POINT`.
Header guards continue subsystem naming styles such as `DOLPHIN_AR_AR_H` for the new `src/dolphin/ar/ar.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added explicit parameter types to `asm` functions such as `DecrementerExceptionHandler(OSException exception, OSContext* context)`, `__OSSetExceptionHandler(OSException, __OSExceptionHandler)`, and `ExternalInterruptHandler(OSException unused, OSContext* ctx)`.
Kept C fallback stubs under `#else` with matching signatures and `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, including `OSGetStackPointer(void)`.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` around an unused 8-byte stack placeholder in `grfigure3.c` to preserve matching behavior only when needed.
Parenthesized a `GXSetDispCopySrc` bitwise expression in `GXFrameBuf.c`, likely making precedence explicit without changing intended register-field logic.
Added dummy parameter names to empty callback bodies, e.g. `__ARQCallbackHack(ARQRequest* arg0) {}` and stage callback no-ops, to keep signatures typed while acknowledging unused values.
review_feedback: One collaborator review by r-burns approved the PR.
No review comment body or inline review feedback was present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish the remaining TExp strict-prototypes warnings mentioned in PR #692.
Search for remaining `()` prototypes after PR #692, especially in headers and local extern declarations.
Audit callback declarations using `Event` or `void (*)()` to see whether they should use `HSD_ObjUpdateFunc` or a more specific typedef.
Check whether `item2.h` later receives a correct typed declaration for the removed `func_8026B7F8`.
Look for later OS header cleanups around `OSException`, `__OSExceptionHandler`, and non-MWERKS fallback stubs.
